BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Rough estimate ahead of the Rosebud fall fair. Working the numbers by hand at the kitchen table on a Sunday afternoon.

CurrentState.Note: Modest quilting income. Heavier months are May through September when fairs run. Fields for rent, utilities, insurance, cleaning, supplies, marketing, instructor pay, drop-ins, equipment sales, membership dues, and owner draw are held at zero because Lisa runs a home-based sole-proprietor quilting practice with no rented studio, no employees, and no membership program.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Placeholder scenario; rent line does not apply to a home-based quilting practice

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Placeholder scenario; rent line does not apply to a home-based quilting practice

Scenario_A_40Percent_SellThrough.Label: Conservative estimate if only 40 percent of the fair inventory sells

Scenario_B_60Percent_SellThrough.Label: Realistic estimate if 60 percent of the fair inventory sells

Scenario_C_80Percent_SellThrough.Label: Best case if 80 percent of the fair inventory sells

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Realistic 60 percent sell through nets around 1380 dollars after the vendor booth fee and the recent fabric outlay. That sits in the 1300 to 1700 window I have in mind.
ActionItems: Confirm the vendor fee at the booth with Wilma before load in
Track cash and card at the fair carefully so the aggregate deposit matches the receipts
Enter the fabric restock receipts as purchase entries against the fabric supplies account in the ledger
